channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Haziribag Man Dies in Lake Fishing Accident
item.description: A tragic incident occurred in Haziribag, Jharkhand, where a man lost his life while fishing in a local lake. Details surrounding the circumstances of the drowning are still emerging, but authorities h...

item.title: Court of Appeal overturns Benjamin Field’s murder conviction of university lecturer Peter Farquhar
item.description: Benjamin Field, a former churchwarden and son of a Baptist minister, has had his 2019 murder conviction quashed by the Court of Appeal. Field had been sentenced to life imprisonment for the killing of...

item.title: Kim Ji Soo Joins Marithé + François Girbaud Philippines as Brand Ambassador
item.description: Filipino fans of Korean entertainment have another reason to celebrate as renowned Korean actor Kim Ji Soo has officially become the latest brand ambassador for Marithé + François Girbaud Philippines....

item.title: MastiFest 2026 Gala Dinner celebrates elegance and cultural flair in Umhlanga
item.description: Masti Productions hosted an evening filled with glamour, sophistication and cultural celebration at the MastiFest 2026 Gala Dinner, held at the Premier Hotel Umhlanga. The black-tie event drew a varie...

item.title: Strengthening Democracy through Comprehensive Voter Education in South Africa
item.description: The article argues that South Africa urgently needs reforms to make voting easier, including automatic voter eligibility at age 18. It stresses the importance of extensive voter education that goes be...

item.title: PM Modi responds to DMK's black attire protest against Delimitation Bill during Women's Reservation Bill discussion
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi addressed the Lok Sabha on Thursday during the special session of Parliament while introducing the Women's Reservation Bill and the Delimitation Bill, 2026. He took a ligh...

item.title: Pastor Kanyari urges inclusivity as Manzi Wa Mombasa exits his church over personal reasons
item.description: Pastor Victor Kanyari has spoken up following Manzi Wa Mombasa’s decision to quit his church, citing her LGBTQ+ identity and the fear of embarrassing him. Manzi Wa Mombasa explained that she’s gratefu...

item.title: Challenges of Post-Release Support Amid Northern Ontario's Drug Crisis
item.description: A report from the John Howard Society of Ontario highlights the heightened risks of drug-related deaths among individuals recently released from correctional facilities in northern Ontario, particular...

item.title: KwaZulu-Natal residents urged to take precautions ahead of severe thunderstorms
item.description: The South African Weather Service has issued a Level 2 weather warning for KwaZulu-Natal, effective from today until late evening. Residents of the province, especially in eThekwini, are advised to ex...

item.title: New Jersey governor blames Fifa as World Cup train fares tipped to hit $100
item.description: New Jersey Governor Mikie Sherrill has accused Fifa of shifting the burden of transport costs onto fans and local taxpayers as World Cup‑related train tickets from New York’s Penn Station to MetLife S...

item.title: Fake quote card falsely attributes statement on fuel prices to CDO Representative Lordan Suan
item.description: Rappler clarified that a circulating quote card allegedly showing Cagayan de Oro 1st District Representative Lordan Suan saying citizens should not worry about rising fuel prices as long as President ...

item.title: SpiceJet and Akasa aircraft sustain minor damages after ground collision at Delhi airport
item.description: On 16th April 2026, a ground collision occurred at Indira Gandhi International Airport in Delhi when a SpiceJet B737-700 aircraft, while taxiing, hit a stationary Akasa Air plane. The impact involved ...

item.title: World Athletics Denies Favour Ofili’s Request To Represent Turkey
item.description: World Athletics don reject the request wey Turkey Athletics Federation submit to make Nigerian sprinter Favour Ofili change her nationality to Turkish ahead of the 2028 Olympics. The Nationality Revie...

item.title: Court of Appeal quashes murder conviction of former church warden jailed for killing university lecturer
item.description: A former church warden who was serving a life sentence for the murder of a university lecturer has had his conviction overturned by the Court of Appeal. Benjamin Field had been jailed for a minimum of...

item.title: Why Dr Disrespect’s Wife Continues to Support Him Despite Past Controversies
item.description: Dr Disrespect’s wife, popularly known online as Mrs. Assassin, continues to support her husband despite his past controversies, including his 2017 infidelity and sending inappropriate messages to a mi...

item.title: Ugandan comedian MC Kapale returns home after struggles and unhappy marriage in the US
item.description: Ugandan comedian Simon Bashir Kawuki, popularly known as MC Kapale, has come back to Kampala after facing tough times in the United States. He had gone there hoping for greener pastures but ended up d...

item.title: Pope Leo denounces tyranny and calls for peace during Africa tour
item.description: During his visit to Cameroon, Pope Leo delivered a powerful message against tyranny, corruption, and the misuse of religion to justify war. Speaking in Bamenda, a city at the center of a violent civil...

item.title: Duke and Duchess of Sussex arrive in Sydney after Melbourne engagements
item.description: The Duke and Duchess of Sussex, Prince Harry and Meghan Markle, have arrived in Sydney after spending time in Melbourne as part of a four-day private visit to Australia. The couple touched down in Syd...

item.title: Fitch warns African banks could face stress if Iran conflict pushes oil prices higher
item.description: Fitch Ratings don warn say African banking system fit dey under pressure if the Iran conflict continue and oil prices rise sharply. For their baseline scenario wey dey assume say the conflict go short...

item.title: Man convicted of murder following fatal stabbing of university student in Twickenham
item.description: Dino Donaldson has been convicted of the murder of 21-year-old accounting student Anojan Gnaneswaran at Strawberry Hill station in Twickenham. The attack occurred following a dispute over a drug deal ...
